zh.lifeSummary: 當Princess Joan Stewart於1428年11月1日出生在蘇格蘭培爾培斯時，她的父親James I Stewart King of Scotland34歲，母親Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland20歲。她於1459年5月15日與James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1493年6月22日在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯享年64歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Princess Joan Stewart was born on 1 November 1428, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, James I Stewart King of Scotland, was 34 and her mother, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, was 20. She married James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas on 15 May 1459, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 22 June 1493, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 64, and was buried in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Princess Joan Stewart est née le 1 novembre 1428, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, James I Stewart King of Scotland, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas le 15 mai 1459, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 juin 1493, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Princess Joan Stewartが1428年11月1日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで生まれた時，父James I Stewart King of Scotlandは34歳，母Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotlandは20歳であった。1459年5月15日にイギリススコットランドでJames Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1493年6月22日にスコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで64歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Princess Joan Stewart(이)가 1428년 11월 1일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James I Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1459년 5월 15일 영국 스코틀랜드에서 James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1493년 6월 22일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Princess Joan Stewart родилась 1 ноября 1428 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени James I Stewart King of Scotland было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas 15 мая 1459 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 22 июня 1493 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.1 November 1428–22 June 1493: 1 de noviembre de 1428–22 de junio de 1493
es.Abercorn, West Lothian, Scotland: Abercorn, West Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Princess Joan Stewart nació el 1 de noviembre de 1428, en Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, su papá, James I Stewart King of Scotland, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland, tenía 20 años. Se casó con James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas el 15 de mayo de 1459, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 22 de junio de 1493, en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Escocia, Reino Unido.
